intro: Ochyrella rossii, also known as O. rossii or Indian Sarsaparilla, is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae, native to India and Southeast Asia, and used in traditional medicine. Ochyrella rossii is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, fever, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ochyrella rossii include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, such as berberine and menispermum alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Ochyrella rossii is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including tinctures, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
